Error: Incorrect CNC machining of slotted holes (affecting 3D adjustability)
Improper bending angle during press brake operation
Inadequate surface protection (e.g., thin galvanization layer)
Poor weld penetration or porosity (for welded steel brackets)
Incorrect or missing attachment of thermal break isolator pads
Burrs or sharp edges left after cutting (poor deburring process)
Laser cutting or CNC punching machine breakdown
Use of incorrect metal grade or alloy in the batch
Dimensional deviation/warping during the cooling phase
Packing of incorrect bracket types (e.g., mixing fixed and sliding dead-load brackets)
